arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha sends Arsenal transfer message with eight-word claim Mikel Arteta will love
arts.sum: Deep into added time, he then unselfishly squared to Hwang Hee-chan when through on goal which allowed the South Korea international to score a second with Cunha's comments on that goal certainly telling of his character as a 'leader of the team'. That's me as a leader of the team and then I give it to Channy [Hwang Hee-chan.] I think it's first goal in the Premier League [this season] and he says every single day 'pass to me please'.

arts.ttl: Arsenal eyeing January move for Premier League star with 64 goals & 41 assists to address injury crisis – Report
arts.sum: Arsenal have identified Wolves striker Matheus Cunha as a potential target to bolster their attacking options in the upcoming January transfer window, according to The Telegraph. The North London club’s interest comes amid an injury crisis, with key forward Bukayo Saka sidelined for several weeks due to a hamstring injury sustained against Crystal Palace last […] The post Arsenal eyeing January move for Premier League star with 64 goals & 41 assists to address injury crisis – Report appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta faces January transfer choice as Arsenal sent clear message
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta faces a January transfer decision over the short-term future of goalkeeper Tommy Setford, with clubs showing an interest in a loan deal. Given the market around goalkeepers in January, it makes it unlikely that Arsenal will be able to bring in the replacement, meaning that Setford is expected to stay with the club.

arts.ttl: What channel is Arsenal vs Ipswich on? How to watch Premier League match for free
arts.sum: The post-Christmas Premier League action concludes with Arsenal taking on Ipswich Town, which also makes it the last game available to watch for free in the UK

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Star's agent arrives to negotiate amid Benjamin Sesko update
arts.sum: Arsenal could be tempted to increase their firepower in front of goal in the January transfer window as they consider how to contend with Bukayo Saka's injury in the second half of the season

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Ipswich: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Arsenal take on Ipswich Town in the Premier League on Friday night. Preview includes team news, predicted lineups, how to watch on TV and more.

arts.ttl: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal eyeing Wolves' Matheus Cunha
arts.sum: Arsenal are looking to sign Wolves' Matheus Cunha to reinforce their attacking line. Transfer Talk has the latest news, gossip and rumors.

arts.ttl: Analysis: How big will Saka's absence be for Arsenal?
arts.sum: Creating 19 'big chances' - six more than anyone else in the Premier League - the wide man also scores highly for assists, touches inside the opposition box, and for key passes, ranking second in the division for those metrics. So much of Arsenal’s best attacking play come down their right flank, with Arteta setting his team up in a way that is designed to get Saka onto the ball in 1v1s as often as possible.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta receives 'positive reports' over £327k Aaron Ramsdale replacement
arts.sum: Arsenal reportedly shortlist 22-year-old Boca Juniors goalkeeper Leandro Brey as a transfer target for the January 2025 window.

arts.ttl: Arteta backs Martinelli to thrive in ‘bigger role’ at Arsenal after Saka injury
arts.sum: Manager plays down claims Arsenal could buy in January Saka out for ‘many weeks’ after injury at Crystal Palace Mikel Arteta is hoping Gabriel Martinelli can help to fill the void created by Bukayo Saka’s hamstring injury, with the England forward likely to miss key fixtures including the north London derby next month. Arteta confirmed on Monday that Saka was expected to be absent for “many weeks” after tearing a hamstring during the first half of Arsenal’s win at Crystal Palace. That would rule him out of Premier League fixtures including Arsenal’s game with Tottenham on 15 January, the Carabao Cup semi‑final first leg against Newcastle and an FA Cup third‑round tie with Manchester United.
